exemplar.title: Why was there a People Power (EDSA) Revolution in the Philippines in 1986?

detailedExemplarData.title: Why was there a People Power (EDSA) Revolution in the Philippines in 1986?

strengths: The research question is concise, sharply focused and consistently guides the investigation throughout.
Methodology demonstrates an informed selection of a wide range of appropriate primary and secondary sources.
The topic is communicated clearly with precise temporal and geographical boundaries, and each chapter ties back to the central enquiry.
weaknesses: The introduction is truncated mid-sentence, disrupting full articulation of the research aim.
Discussion of methodological limitations (e.g. interview bias, journalistic source constraints) is acknowledged only briefly and could be deeper.
A few minor formatting/quotation truncations (page 18) detract from the otherwise seamless focus.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall, the student meets the descriptors at the top band for Focus and Method in terms of clear communication, sharply focused research question, and appropriately justified methodology. However, minor lapses in the introductionâs completeness and only brief methodological critique prevent a flawless performance, warranting a score of 5 out of 6.

strengths: Source material (primary and secondary) is clearly relevant and integrated effectively to answer the research question.
Demonstrates sophisticated and coherent subject knowledge, weaving quantitative data and historiographical debate into analysis.
Subject-specific terminology and concepts are used accurately and consistently throughout.
weaknesses: Occasional over-reliance on journalistic reportage without full corroboration.
One key statistic (two-million participants) is uncited, slightly weakening source reliability assessment.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates full command of relevant evidence, coherent historical understanding, and consistent use of technical terminology, satisfying the top-band descriptors for Knowledge and Understanding. Minor lapses in source corroboration and citation prevent an absolutely flawless presentation but do not detract from overall excellence, justifying a score of 6 out of 6.

strengths: Research base is extensive and judiciously selected, employing triangulation across economic, political and religious dimensions.
Discussion develops a reasoned, coherent argument that weighs counter-arguments and culminates in a nuanced conclusion prioritising Aquinoâs assassination.
Analysis moves beyond description to synthesize causal chains with mini-conclusions that link evidence to the research question.
weaknesses: Some analytical assertions (debt figures, protest numbers) lack sufficient cross-checking or statistical corroboration.
Evaluation of certain evidence (e.g. pastoral lettersâ differential reception, election-rigging statistics) is uneven and could be deepened.
A few argumentative threads end abruptly, preventing fully developed critical evaluation in places.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates strong critical thinking: a consistently relevant research base, well-structured argument, and meaningful evaluation of sources. However, uneven depth in cross-checking data and incomplete analytical threads place it in the 7â9 band rather than the topmost band. A score of 7 out of 12 reflects this strong but not fully flawless performance.

strengths: All required structural elements (title page, contents, chapters, bibliography) are present and logically ordered.
Layout and formatting (font, spacing, headings, page numbering, citation style) are consistently applied and enhance readability.
Word count and session details are clearly stated; no compulsory elements are missing under the current guide.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation fully meets the highest-band descriptors: the structure is entirely appropriate and all layout considerations are correctly applied. There are no substantive formatting issues, justifying a top score of 4 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Consistent font, spacing, heading hierarchy, page numbering and citation style (author-date) are maintained throughout, and the word count plus session details are clearly stated on the title page.  Minor formatting issues (e.g., occasional line-break artifacts in the submission extract) do not impede readability.  Overall, layout and formal presentation meet the highest band for this criterion.

The essay presents a clear, largely coherent argument that evaluates multiple causes of the revolution and culminates in a nuanced judgement prioritising Ninoy Aquinoâs assassination.  Counter-arguments (e.g., economic long-term factors, Church leadership) are considered and weighed, evidencing critical engagement.  Evaluation of sourcesâsuch as acknowledging interview bias and conflicting economic statisticsâis present but not uniformly thorough, and some evidence (e.g., size of protest) is insufficiently scrutinised.  The work thus meets the descriptors for the 7-9 band, earning a mid-band score of 8.

The topic is stated succinctly in the title and reiterated in the introduction with clear temporal (1972â1986) and geographical (Philippines) boundaries.  The student justifies the investigationâs relevance by linking it to ongoing historical debate and contemporary political discourse, demonstrating an excellent sense of purpose.  Each chapter is explicitly tied to the overarching enquiry into the causes of the 1986 People Power Revolution, showing a consistently sharp research focus.  The clarity, precision and sustained appropriateness of the topic fully meet the descriptors for the top band.

All required structural components (title page, contents, introduction, body chapters, conclusion, bibliography) are present and logically ordered.  Chapter and sub-section divisions guide the reader, and mini-conclusions aid coherence.  Although the absence of an abstract is noted, this element is no longer compulsory under the current EE Guide, so structure remains fully appropriate.  The work therefore satisfies the top band for this strand.

The investigation draws on a wide range of primary (interview, pastoral letters, government documents, contemporary media, archival statistics) and secondary sources (academic monographs, journal articles, economic reports).  The student explains why each body of evidence is pertinentâfor example, interviewing Angela Stuart-Santiago for insights into propaganda and using Sicatâs economic analyses for debt dataâindicating informed selection.  Minor weaknesses remain: the limitations of journalistic sources and interview bias are acknowledged only briefly, and opportunities to incorporate additional archival or governmental materials are noted but not realised.  These reservations prevent the work from reaching flawless completeness, positioning it securely at the lower end of the top band.

The research base is extensive and judiciously selected.  Primary evidence (interviews, official documents, speeches, statistical data) and high-quality secondary scholarship are employed across all chapters.  Sources are current where appropriate and span economic, political and religious dimensions, ensuring triangulation of perspectives.  A handful of claims rely on journalistic reportage without corroboration, but overall the breadth, relevance and integration of research meet the 10â12 band, meriting a score of 10.

Analysis is consistently linked to the research question and moves beyond description to weigh the relative significance of each causal factor.  The student constructs logical chainsâe.g., crony capitalism â economic decline â social unrestâand employs quantitative data to support claims.  Mini-conclusions synthesise findings effectively.  Nonetheless, some assertions (e.g., precise debt figures, mobilisation numbers) are accepted without sufficient cross-checking, and theoretical frameworks (Kershaw) are not fully interrogated, leaving the depth just short of the highest band.  An 8 reflects solid, largely well-supported analytical work within the 7-9 range.

The essay consistently deploys highly relevant primary and secondary sourcesâeconomic statistics, pastoral letters, Supreme Court rulings, eyewitness interviewsâto substantiate each claim.  Evidence is well integrated into the argument rather than merely cited, and the student shows an awareness of provenance and purpose (e.g., discussing propaganda value of government documents).  Occasional over-reliance on journalistic reportage and one uncited statistic (two-million participants) slightly diminish the overall strength, hence a score of 5 rather than 6.

The narrative demonstrates command of complex historical content: economic policy under Marcos, dynamics of martial-law politics, the role of Catholicism in Filipino society and theoretical perspectives on agency.  The student weaves factual detail, quantitative data and historiographical debate into a coherent analytical account, showing sophisticated understanding of both the period and wider historical discourse.  Misinterpretations are rare and minor, so the descriptor for full marks is satisfied.

The research questionââWhy was there a People Power (EDSA) Revolution in the Philippines in 1986?ââis concise, sharply delimited and explicitly causal.  Throughout the essay the student keeps returning to this guiding question, using mini-conclusions and a summative synthesis to evaluate the weight of each factor.  There is no drift into peripheral issues, and subsidiary questions (economic, political, individual and religious factors) are clearly subordinated to the central line of enquiry, satisfying the highest descriptor level for clarity and focus.

Terminology such as âcrony capitalism,â âsnap election,â âmartial law,â âpastoral letters,â and âcausationâ is used accurately and consistently.  Concepts from historiography (e.g., Kershawâs views on individual agency) are correctly applied to the Philippine context, evidencing confident command of subject-specific language.  No significant misuse or omission of key terms is evident, justifying the top band and full score.

annotations.comment: The sugar industry case is compelling but lacks follow-through; the paragraph ends abruptly, preventing full evaluation of its impact on the argument.
The use of quantitative figures to critique the Bataan Nuclear Power Plant demonstrates solid analytical depth and strengthens the causal linkage between failed projects and debt accumulation.
The introduction cuts off mid-sentence, suggesting a truncation or formatting issue that disrupts the readerâs understanding of the complete research aim.
The methodological overview clearly identifies a mixed primary and secondary source approach, demonstrating informed selection of evidence.
While the methodology lists diverse sources, the discussion of limitations for interview data is brief; a deeper critique of potential bias would strengthen methodological rigor.
The introductory paragraph sets context but omits explicit sub-questions for this chapter, reducing clarity on how economic conditions are specifically interrogated.
The analysis of crony capitalism is supported by judicial and institutional sources, illustrating effective application of relevant evidence.
The discussion of loyalist perspectives could benefit from contrasting data or scholarly critique to avoid uncritical acceptance of retrospective âGolden Ageâ narratives.
The account of the 1972 bombings could be strengthened by referencing primary archival reports or corroborating eyewitness records to validate the strategic narrative.
The Manila Film Centre case is used persuasively to illustrate media manipulation, showing coherent subject understanding and effective application of concept.
The research question is concise, sharply focused and clearly defines the inquiry into the People Power Revolution, effectively guiding the investigation.
The explicit word count is clearly stated and confirms adherence to the maximum word limit requirement.
The table of contents is well-structured and aligns chapters and page numbers logically, enhancing document navigability.
The introduction restates the research question prominently, reinforcing focus and establishing clear investigative direction.
The account of Imelda Marcosâs spending is vivid and well-supported by diverse sources, illustrating coherent subject knowledge on wealth disparity.
The transition to quality-of-life discussion lacks explicit linkage to statistical evidence; integrating specific inflation or income data here would enhance the argument.
The inclusion of international trade and inflation statistics provides depth to the analysis, demonstrating strong triangulation of economic indicators.
The figures on âill-gotten wealthâ are compelling but lack citation of legal text or court documents for verification, weakening source reliability assessment.
The mini-conclusion effectively synthesizes the economic chapterâs findings and acknowledges the necessity of political factors, maintaining clear focus on the research question.
The title succinctly states the investigationâs focus, providing clear context and scope.
The contextual overview of martial law and its relevance is thorough, demonstrating strong subject knowledge and effectively setting the stage for analysis.
The transition to political conditions is coherent and maintains logical structure, guiding the reader smoothly into the next line of inquiry.
The paragraph presents robust statistics on human rights abuses, demonstrating effective use of quantitative evidence to illustrate the regimeâs coercion.
This section conflates human rights abuses with political opposition tactics; disentangling these elements would clarify causal links and strengthen the argument.
The concluding summary cogently argues how cycles of repression fueled opposition, showing coherent evaluative thinking and supported conclusions.
The final sentence trails off mid-quote; completing the quotation or paraphrasing fully would enhance rhetorical closure.
The nuanced argument distinguishing structural and individual factors demonstrates strong critical thinking and well-supported evaluative judgment.
The reflection on why the revolution occurred in 1986 rather than earlier could benefit from deeper analysis of intervening events or missed opportunities.
The bibliography is introduced with a clear heading, adhering to structural conventions and facilitating easy reference.
References are generally consistent but some sources lack access dates or DOIs; applying uniform citation completeness would enhance presentation.
The narrative on Ninoy Aquino is well-supported by primary and secondary accounts, demonstrating strong research quality and relevance to the central question.
The description of election rigging lacks explicit statistical data on vote counts or independent observer reports; integrating these would add credibility to the claims.
The engagement with Kershawâs historiographical debate provides a solid conceptual framework, demonstrating accurate use of subject-specific terminology.
The quote on âpeople power phenomenonâ is cut mid-sentence, disrupting the flow and preventing full interpretation of the evidence.
The use of Randy Davidâs eyewitness account of the funeral effectively illustrates mass mobilization, enhancing analytical depth.
The summary ends abruptly, lacking a complete closing sentence; revising to include a conclusive statement would improve coherence.
This reflective paragraph acknowledges the interplay between individual agency and structural conditions, demonstrating nuanced critical evaluation.
The statistical evidence on Catholic adherence effectively establishes relevance, demonstrating clear understanding of demographic context.
The analysis of pastoral letters is descriptive; evaluating the lettersâ reception or impact on different social groups would strengthen the critical evaluation.
The precise recount of Cardinal Sinâs broadcast demonstrates effective selection of primary evidence and clear linkage to mobilization outcomes.
The cited two-million figure lacks reference to methodology; indicating the sourceâs estimation method would improve the reliability assessment.
The conclusion for the churchâs role synthesizes multiple factors and articulates its unifying effect, showing coherent argumentation.
The conclusion effectively summarizes the multi-factor model, reinforcing the original research question and maintaining focus.
